---
title: "Who owns India's plant varieties?"
subtitle: "Explore the PPV&FRA certification data"
---

::: {style="margin-top:-0.75rem;margin-bottom:1.5rem"}
[![DOI](https://zenodo.org/badge/1112427994.svg)](https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.21140353)
:::

```{r setup}
source("R/prep_dashboard.R")

register_start_year <- min(d$issue_year, na.rm = TRUE)

trends_df <- chr(sel_for(dd) %>% filter(!is.na(sector)) %>%
  count(sel, issue_year, sector, name = "n"))

comp_all <- d %>% filter(!is.na(sector)) %>% count(sector, name = "n") %>% mutate(sel = "All crops")
comp_df  <- chr(bind_rows(comp_all, sel_for(d) %>% filter(!is.na(sector)) %>% count(sel, sector, name = "n")))

firm_key    <- sel_for(d) %>% filter(applicant_category == "Private") %>%
  mutate(firm = alias_company(canon_company(applicant)))
firm_labels <- firm_key %>% count(firm, applicant) %>% group_by(firm) %>%
  slice_max(n, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>% ungroup() %>%
  transmute(firm, label = coalesce(unname(pretty_names[firm]), applicant))
firm_df <- chr(firm_key %>% count(sel, firm, name = "n") %>%
  group_by(sel) %>% slice_max(n, n = 10, with_ties = FALSE) %>% ungroup() %>%
  left_join(firm_labels, by = "firm"))

var_df <- chr(sel_for(d) %>% filter(!is.na(variety_category), !is.na(sector)) %>%
  count(sel, sector, variety_category, name = "n"))

cum_sel <- sel_for(dd) %>% filter(!is.na(sector)) %>% count(sel, issue_year, sector, name = "n")
cum_all <- dd %>% filter(!is.na(sector)) %>% count(issue_year, sector, name = "n") %>%
  mutate(sel = "All crops")
cum_df  <- chr(bind_rows(cum_all, cum_sel) %>% arrange(sel, sector, issue_year) %>%
  group_by(sel, sector) %>% mutate(cum = cumsum(n)) %>% ungroup())

# every crop x applicant category, uncapped, for the reference heatmap
heat_df <- chr(d %>% filter(!is.na(crop), !is.na(sector)) %>% count(crop, sector, name = "n"))

# firm x crop table for the searchable lookup: every private applicant, every
# crop they hold certificates in, and their share of private certificates
# within that crop specifically
firm_crop_key <- d %>% filter(applicant_category == "Private", !is.na(crop)) %>%
  mutate(key = alias_company(canon_company(applicant)))
firm_crop_labels <- firm_crop_key %>% count(key, applicant) %>% group_by(key) %>%
  slice_max(n, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>% ungroup() %>%
  transmute(key, firm = coalesce(unname(pretty_names[key]), applicant))
crop_priv_totals <- d %>% filter(applicant_category == "Private", !is.na(crop)) %>%
  count(crop, name = "crop_total")
firm_totals <- firm_crop_key %>% count(key, name = "total")
firm_table_df <- chr(firm_crop_key %>% count(key, crop, name = "certs") %>%
  left_join(firm_crop_labels, by = "key") %>%
  left_join(firm_totals, by = "key") %>%
  left_join(crop_priv_totals, by = "crop") %>%
  mutate(share_of_firm = round(100 * certs / total, 1),
         share_of_crop = round(100 * certs / crop_total, 1)) %>%
  transmute(Firm = firm, Crop = crop, Certificates = certs,
            `Total (all crops)` = total,
            `% of this firm's own certificates` = share_of_firm,
            `% of all private certificates in this crop` = share_of_crop) %>%
  arrange(desc(Certificates)))

# Ranked overview of every private firm across all crops combined. Feeds the
# firm-lookup combobox's name list and its rank/total/crop-count summary.
# Reuses top_firms_ranked() (same helper the charts use) rather than a
# separate ranking pass. Not sliced to a top-N, since a single search box
# should be able to find any firm, not just the largest ones.
firm_n_crops <- firm_crop_key %>% distinct(key, crop) %>% count(key, name = "n_crops")
firm_overview_df <- chr(top_firms_ranked(d) %>%
  left_join(firm_n_crops, by = "key") %>%
  transmute(Rank = row_number(), Firm = label, `Total certificates` = n,
            `Crops active in` = n_crops,
            `Share of all private certificates (%)` = round(pct, 1)))

# Gini lookup by crop, computed once at render time (not recomputed per
# dropdown click). Reuses gini() and the same canon_company()/alias_company()
# merge already built above via firm_crop_key -- no separate dedup logic.
firm_crop_counts <- firm_crop_key %>% count(key, crop, name = "certs")
gini_by_crop <- firm_crop_counts %>%
  group_by(crop) %>%
  summarise(n_firms = n_distinct(key), gini = gini(certs), .groups = "drop") %>%
  filter(n_firms >= 5)

gini_bar_df <- chr(firm_crop_counts %>%
  semi_join(gini_by_crop, by = "crop") %>%
  left_join(firm_crop_labels, by = "key") %>%
  group_by(crop) %>% slice_max(certs, n = 10, with_ties = FALSE) %>% ungroup() %>%
  transmute(crop, firm, certs))

gini_default_crop <- gini_by_crop %>% filter(str_detect(str_to_lower(crop), "tetraploid")) %>%
  pull(crop) %>% first()
if (is.na(gini_default_crop)) {
  gini_default_crop <- first(gini_by_crop$crop[str_to_lower(gini_by_crop$crop) == "cotton"],
                             default = sort(gini_by_crop$crop)[1])
}

ojs_define(categories_ojs = crop_categories)
ojs_define(crops_ojs      = top_crops)
ojs_define(trends_ojs = trends_df)
ojs_define(comp_ojs   = comp_df)
ojs_define(firm_ojs   = firm_df)
ojs_define(var_ojs    = var_df)
ojs_define(cum_ojs    = cum_df)
ojs_define(heat_ojs       = heat_df)
ojs_define(firm_table_ojs = firm_table_df)
ojs_define(firm_overview_ojs = firm_overview_df)
ojs_define(gini_crops_ojs      = sort(gini_by_crop$crop))
ojs_define(gini_lookup_ojs     = chr(gini_by_crop))
ojs_define(gini_bar_ojs        = gini_bar_df)
ojs_define(gini_default_crop_ojs = gini_default_crop)
```

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal"}
**`r format(n_total, big.mark=",")` certificates** analysed, from the
[PPV&FR Authority register](https://plantauthority.gov.in/list-certificates).
Last data refresh: **`r last_updated`**. This page rebuilds every week.
:::

Every chart below answers the same set of questions for whichever crop you pick: who holds
certificates, is the market concentrated, and how has that changed over time. For the full
write-up, background, and the cotton case study, see [Full analysis](analysis.qmd).

::: {.callout-note appearance="minimal" icon="false"}
**About this data.** A certificate under the PPVFRA confers intellectual property rights
over a registered plant variety: the right to control commercial sale, reproduction, and
licensing for a fixed term. This register records certificates granted, not applications
filed or rejected. Certificates are issued to five applicant categories: Private firms,
State Agricultural Universities (SAUs), Public institutions, Farmers, and Individual
Breeders.
:::

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" appearance="simple"}
## Methodology and data notes

**Source.** The PPV&FR Authority's public certificate register:
[plantauthority.gov.in/list-certificates](https://plantauthority.gov.in/list-certificates).

**Update frequency.** Weekly, every Sunday, via an automated pipeline that re-scrapes the
full register, rebuilds the dataset, and republishes this site.

**Coverage.** Every certificate granted since `r register_start_year`, through the most
recent successful weekly refresh.

**What's excluded.** Applications that were withdrawn or rejected are not in this register
and are not represented here. This page only covers certificates that were actually
granted.

**Known limitations.** Company names are merged across spelling variants using a
name-matching heuristic (`canon_company()` / `alias_company()`), not a corporate-registry
lookup, so a genuine typo or an unlisted acronym could still cause one real firm to appear
as two. Full methodology, including how every statistic on this page is computed, is
documented in the [project repository](https://github.com/ambika-subash/pvp-certification-explorer#readme).
:::

::: {.callout-important appearance="simple"}
The following charts are interactive. Use the dropdowns to filter by crop, crop category,
or applicant category.
:::

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
categories = categories_ojs
crops = crops_ojs
sectorDomain = ["Farmer", "Private", "Public", "SAU", "Individual Breeder"]
sectorRange  = ["#4daf4a", "#377eb8", "#e41a1c", "#ff7f00", "#984ea3"]
catDomain = ["Farmer", "New", "Extant", "Extant (Notified)", "Extant (VCK)", "EDV"]
catRange  = ["#4daf4a", "#377eb8", "#80b1d3", "#ff7f00", "#e41a1c", "#984ea3"]

// Custom paired viewof: a category dropdown and a crop dropdown side by side.
// Whichever one you touch becomes active; the other disables and greys out
// so there's no ambiguity about which selection the chart is using.
function pairedCropInput({catOptions = categories, cropOptions = crops,
                           catValue, cropValue, catLabel, cropLabel,
                           defaultMode = "crop"} = {}) {
  const catSel  = Inputs.select(catOptions,  {label: catLabel  ?? "Crop category", value: catValue  ?? catOptions[0]});
  const cropSel = Inputs.select(cropOptions, {label: cropLabel ?? "Specific crop", value: cropValue ?? cropOptions[0]});
  const box = html`<div style="display:flex;gap:2rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:flex-end;margin-bottom:0.5rem">
    <div>${catSel}</div><div>${cropSel}</div></div>`;

  // Both dropdowns stay fully clickable at all times. "Disabled" here is
  // purely visual (dimmed) so the inactive one can still be clicked to
  // switch modes back. A truly HTML-disabled <select> can't be re-enabled
  // by clicking it, which is why that approach doesn't work here.
  function setMode(mode) {
    const catActive = mode === "category";
    catSel.style.opacity  = catActive ? "1" : "0.45";
    cropSel.style.opacity = catActive ? "0.45" : "1";
    box.value = catActive ? catSel.value : cropSel.value;
    box.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", {bubbles: true}));
  }

  catSel.addEventListener("input",  (e) => { e.stopPropagation(); setMode("category"); });
  cropSel.addEventListener("input", (e) => { e.stopPropagation(); setMode("crop"); });
  setMode(defaultMode === "crop" ? "crop" : "category");
  return box;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Inputs.table always shows a row-selection checkbox column, which none of
// the tables on this page use (they're for browsing/searching, not feeding
// a selection into another chart). Injected once, applies to every table
// with this class regardless of where it appears on the page.
html`<style>
  .firm-lookup-table :is(th, td):first-child { display: none; }
</style>`
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
// Shared helpers: turn any crop/category selection into a filename-safe
// slug, and let a chart's SVG be saved as a PNG. Plot.plot() returns a bare
// <svg> for simple charts but a <figure> wrapping an <svg> when a legend is
// present, so downloadChartAsPng() checks for both.
function slug(s) {
  return String(s).toLowerCase().replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, "_").replace(/^_+|_+$/g, "");
}

function downloadChartAsPng(chartNode, filename) {
  const svgEl = chartNode instanceof SVGElement ? chartNode : chartNode.querySelector("svg");
  if (!svgEl) return;
  const scale = 2;
  const w = svgEl.width && svgEl.width.baseVal ? svgEl.width.baseVal.value : svgEl.clientWidth;
  const h = svgEl.height && svgEl.height.baseVal ? svgEl.height.baseVal.value : svgEl.clientHeight;
  const svgString = new XMLSerializer().serializeToString(svgEl);
  const svgBlob = new Blob([svgString], {type: "image/svg+xml;charset=utf-8"});
  const url = URL.createObjectURL(svgBlob);
  const img = new Image();
  img.onload = () => {
    const canvas = document.createElement("canvas");
    canvas.width = w * scale;
    canvas.height = h * scale;
    const ctx = canvas.getContext("2d");
    ctx.scale(scale, scale);
    ctx.fillStyle = "white";
    ctx.fillRect(0, 0, w, h);
    ctx.drawImage(img, 0, 0, w, h);
    URL.revokeObjectURL(url);
    canvas.toBlob((blob) => {
      const a = document.createElement("a");
      a.href = URL.createObjectURL(blob);
      a.download = filename.endsWith(".png") ? filename : filename + ".png";
      document.body.appendChild(a);
      a.click();
      a.remove();
      URL.revokeObjectURL(a.href);
    });
  };
  img.src = url;
}

// Returns a div containing a "Save chart as PNG" button plus the chart
// itself, so the two always sit together wherever this is used.
function withExport(chartNode, filename) {
  const btn = html`<button style="margin-bottom:0.5rem;padding:4px 12px;font-size:12px;
    border:1px solid #d1d5db;border-radius:4px;background:#f9fafb;cursor:pointer">
    ⬇ Save chart as PNG</button>`;
  btn.onclick = () => downloadChartAsPng(chartNode, filename);
  return html`<div>${btn}<div>${chartNode}</div></div>`;
}
```

## Cumulative ownership over time

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sel5 = pairedCropInput({
  catOptions: ["All crops"].concat(categories), catValue: "All crops",
  cropValue: "Cotton", defaultMode: "category"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof yr5 = Inputs.range(
  [2009, d3.max(transpose(cum_ojs), d => d.issue_year)],
  {step: 1, label: "Up to year",
   value: d3.max(transpose(cum_ojs), d => d.issue_year)})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
rows5 = transpose(cum_ojs).filter(d => d.sel === sel5 && d.issue_year <= yr5)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
chartCum = Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 65,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: "Year", tickFormat: "d", tickPadding: 8},
  y: {label: "Cumulative certificates", grid: true, tickPadding: 8},
  color: {legend: true, domain: sectorDomain, range: sectorRange},
  marks: [
    Plot.line(rows5, {x: "issue_year", y: "cum", stroke: "sector", z: "sector"}),
    Plot.dot(rows5, {x: "issue_year", y: "cum", fill: "sector", r: 3, tip: true}),
    Plot.ruleY([0])
  ]
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
withExport(chartCum, `cumulative_ownership_${slug(sel5)}_to_${yr5}`)
```

Private firms have dominated cumulative certifications throughout the register's history.
The gap between private and all other categories has widened consistently since 2014,
driven largely by a surge in cotton registrations. Drag the year slider to watch this
build up rather than just seeing the endpoint. This sets the broadest context, all crops,
all time, before the sections below narrow in on specific crops and firms.

## Every crop at a glance

### Select crops to compare

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
allCrops = Array.from(new Set(transpose(heat_ojs).map(d => d.crop))).sort()
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof cropsPick = Inputs.select(allCrops, {multiple: true, size: 8,
  label: "Crops (click, or Ctrl/Cmd-click for more than one)", value: ["Cotton", "Rice", "Wheat"]})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof modeHeat = Inputs.radio(["Percentage", "Counts"], {label: "Show", value: "Percentage"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
rowsHeat = transpose(heat_ojs).filter(d => cropsPick.includes(d.crop))
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
chartCompare = rowsHeat.length === 0 ? null : Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 110,
  marginBottom: 45,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: modeHeat === "Percentage" ? "Share of certificates" : "Certificates",
      percent: modeHeat === "Percentage", grid: true, tickPadding: 8},
  y: {label: null, tickPadding: 8},
  color: {legend: true, domain: sectorDomain, range: sectorRange},
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(rowsHeat, {x: "n", y: "crop", fill: "sector",
      offset: modeHeat === "Percentage" ? "normalize" : null, tip: true}),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
chartCompare === null
  ? html`<p><em>Select at least one crop above.</em></p>`
  : withExport(chartCompare, `crop_comparison_${cropsPick.map(slug).join("_")}_${slug(modeHeat)}`)
```

Select one or more crops to compare their applicant composition. Toggle between raw
counts and percentage shares. All crops in the register are available here.

## Certificate trends by crop

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sel1 = pairedCropInput({catValue: "Cereals", cropValue: "Cotton"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
chartTrends = Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 65,
  marginBottom: 55,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: "Year", tickFormat: "d", tickRotate: -45, tickPadding: 8},
  y: {label: "Certificates", grid: true, tickPadding: 8},
  color: {legend: true, domain: sectorDomain, range: sectorRange},
  marks: [
    Plot.barY(transpose(trends_ojs).filter(d => d.sel === sel1),
      {x: "issue_year", y: "n", fill: "sector", tip: true}),
    Plot.ruleY([0])
  ]
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
withExport(chartTrends, `certificate_trends_crop_${slug(sel1)}`)
```

Annual certificates for the selected crop, stacked by applicant category.

## Applicant composition by crop

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sel2 = pairedCropInput({catValue: "Cereals", cropValue: "Cotton"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof mode2 = Inputs.radio(["Percentage", "Counts"], {label: "Show", value: "Percentage"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
rows2 = {
  const all = transpose(comp_ojs).filter(d => d.sel === "All crops").map(d => ({...d, group: "All crops"}));
  const sel = transpose(comp_ojs).filter(d => d.sel === sel2).map(d => ({...d, group: sel2}));
  return all.concat(sel);
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
chartComposition = Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 100,
  marginBottom: 45,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: mode2 === "Percentage" ? "Share of certificates" : "Certificates",
      percent: mode2 === "Percentage", grid: true, tickPadding: 8},
  y: {label: null, tickPadding: 8},
  color: {legend: true, domain: sectorDomain, range: sectorRange},
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(rows2, {x: "n", y: "group", fill: "sector",
      offset: mode2 === "Percentage" ? "normalize" : null, tip: true}),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
withExport(chartComposition, `applicant_composition_crop_${slug(sel2)}_${slug(mode2)}`)
```

Two bars: all crops on top, your selection below. Switch to Percentage to compare shape
rather than scale.

## Firm-level concentration

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sel3 = pairedCropInput({catValue: "Cereals", cropValue: "Cotton"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
chartFirms = Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 220,
  marginRight: 30,
  marginBottom: 45,
  width: 760,
  style: {fontSize: "13px"},
  x: {label: "Certificates", grid: true, tickPadding: 8},
  y: {label: null, tickPadding: 8},
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(transpose(firm_ojs).filter(d => d.sel === sel3),
      {x: "n", y: "label", sort: {y: "x", reverse: true}, fill: "#377eb8", tip: true}),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
withExport(chartFirms, `firm_concentration_crop_${slug(sel3)}`)
```

The top ten private applicants for the selected crop.

## Private sector concentration by crop

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
giniCrops = gini_crops_ojs
giniLookup = transpose(gini_lookup_ojs)
giniDefaultCrop = Array.isArray(gini_default_crop_ojs) ? gini_default_crop_ojs[0] : gini_default_crop_ojs
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof giniCrop = Inputs.select(giniCrops, {label: "Crop", value: giniDefaultCrop})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
giniRow   = giniLookup.find(d => d.crop === giniCrop)
giniValue = giniRow ? +giniRow.gini : null
giniLabel = giniValue === null ? ""
  : giniValue > 0.6 ? "High concentration"
  : giniValue >= 0.4 ? "Moderate concentration"
  : "Low concentration"
giniBarRows = transpose(gini_bar_ojs).filter(d => d.crop === giniCrop)
  .sort((a, b) => d3.descending(+a.certs, +b.certs))
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
chartGini = Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 220,
  marginRight: 40,
  marginBottom: 45,
  width: 640,
  height: giniBarRows.length * 32 + 80,
  style: {fontSize: "12px"},
  x: {label: "Certificates", grid: true, tickPadding: 8},
  y: {label: null, tickPadding: 8},
  marks: [
    Plot.barX(giniBarRows, {x: "certs", y: "firm", sort: {y: "x", reverse: true}, fill: "#377eb8"}),
    Plot.text(giniBarRows, {x: "certs", y: "firm", text: "certs", dx: 6, textAnchor: "start"}),
    Plot.ruleX([0])
  ]
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
html`<div style="display:flex;gap:2.5rem;flex-wrap:wrap;align-items:flex-start">
  <div>${withExport(chartGini, `gini_concentration_crop_${slug(giniCrop)}`)}</div>
  <div style="flex:0 0 220px;text-align:center;padding-top:1rem">
    <div style="font-size:3rem;font-weight:700;color:#377eb8">${giniValue === null ? "N/A" : giniValue.toFixed(2)}</div>
    <div style="color:#6b7280;font-size:0.85rem;margin-top:0.25rem">0 = perfectly equal, 1 = one firm holds everything</div>
    <div style="font-weight:600;margin-top:0.75rem">${giniLabel}</div>
  </div>
</div>`
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
giniHighShare = {
  const eligible = giniLookup.filter(d => d.gini !== "" && d.gini != null && !Number.isNaN(+d.gini));
  const high = eligible.filter(d => +d.gini > 0.6);
  return eligible.length > 0 ? high.length / eligible.length : null;
}
giniInterp = giniValue === null ? "" : giniValue > 0.6
  ? "A Gini above 0.6 indicates high concentration: a small number of large seed companies dominate this crop."
  : giniValue >= 0.4
  ? "A Gini between 0.4 and 0.6 indicates moderate concentration: certificates are split among a wider set of firms than in a highly concentrated crop, but still unevenly."
  : "A Gini below 0.4 indicates relatively low concentration: certificates are spread more evenly across private firms in this crop."
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
md`${giniInterp}${giniHighShare === null ? "" : ` Across all eligible crops, ${Math.round(giniHighShare * 100)}% fall in the high-concentration range (Gini above 0.6).`}`
```

Gini coefficient measures concentration among private firms holding at least one
certificate in the selected crop. Firms with no registrations in that crop are excluded.
Crops with fewer than 5 private certificate holders are not shown.

## Variety category breakdown

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sel4 = pairedCropInput({catValue: "Cereals", cropValue: "Cotton"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof sec4 = Inputs.select(["All"].concat(sectorDomain), {label: "Applicant", value: "Private"})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
rows4 = {
  let r = transpose(var_ojs).filter(d => d.sel === sel4);
  if (sec4 !== "All") r = r.filter(d => d.sector === sec4);
  return r;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
chartVariety = Plot.plot({
  marginLeft: 65,
  marginBottom: 80,
  style: {fontSize: "14px"},
  x: {label: null, tickRotate: -30, tickPadding: 8},
  y: {label: "Certificates", grid: true, tickPadding: 8},
  color: {legend: true, domain: catDomain, range: catRange},
  marks: [
    Plot.barY(rows4, {x: "variety_category", y: "n", fill: "variety_category", tip: true}),
    Plot.ruleY([0])
  ]
})
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
withExport(chartVariety, `variety_category_crop_${slug(sel4)}_${slug(sec4)}`)
```

Certificates split across variety categories for the selected crop and applicant. Try
Private together with Cotton.

## Look up a firm

Search any firm, or click one of the largest below to jump straight in.

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
firmOverviewRows = transpose(firm_overview_ojs)
firmNames = firmOverviewRows.map(d => d.Firm).sort()
featuredFirms = firmOverviewRows.slice(0, 6).map(d => d.Firm)

// Default the lookup to Nuziveedu so the panel loads populated rather than
// empty, giving a first-time visitor a worked example straight away. Falls
// back to the largest firm if no Nuziveedu entry is present in the data.
defaultFirm = firmNames.find(n => /nuziveedu/i.test(n)) ?? firmNames[0]

// A native browser combobox: a text input with a <datalist> of suggestions.
// Typing filters the dropdown automatically (built into the browser, no
// custom filtering logic needed), and it still accepts free text. The
// datalist dropdown itself only appears on click/keystroke though (browsers
// don't show it automatically, and Chrome/Firefox/Safari don't even agree
// on when it appears), so a row of always-visible "featured firm" chips
// sits below the input, real names on the page immediately, no interaction
// required to notice them, clicking one fills the box and looks it up.
function firmCombobox(names, featured, initial) {
  const listId = "firm-datalist-" + Math.random().toString(36).slice(2);
  const input = html`<input type="text" list="${listId}" placeholder="Search or select a firm..."
    value="${initial ?? ""}"
    style="padding:6px 10px;font-size:14px;width:340px;border:1px solid #d1d5db;border-radius:4px">`;
  const options = names.map(n => html`<option value="${n}">`);
  const datalist = html`<datalist id="${listId}">${options}</datalist>`;

  function fire() {
    box.value = input.value;
    box.dispatchEvent(new CustomEvent("input", {bubbles: true}));
  }
  input.addEventListener("input", fire);

  const chips = featured.map(name => {
    const chip = html`<button type="button" style="margin:0 6px 6px 0;padding:3px 11px;font-size:12px;
      border:1px solid #d1d5db;border-radius:999px;background:#f3f4f6;color:#374151;cursor:pointer">${name}</button>`;
    chip.addEventListener("click", () => { input.value = name; fire(); });
    return chip;
  });

  const box = html`<div>
    <div>${input}${datalist}</div>
    <div style="margin-top:8px">
      <span style="font-size:12px;color:#6b7280;margin-right:2px">Try:</span> ${chips}
    </div>
  </div>`;
  box.value = initial ?? "";
  return box;
}
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
viewof firmPick = firmCombobox(firmNames, featuredFirms, defaultFirm)
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
firmMatch = firmOverviewRows.find(d => d.Firm.toLowerCase() === firmPick.trim().toLowerCase())
firmCropRows = firmMatch
  ? transpose(firm_table_ojs).filter(d => d.Firm === firmMatch.Firm)
      .sort((a, b) => d3.descending(+a.Certificates, +b.Certificates))
  : []
```

```{ojs}
//| echo: false
firmPick.trim() === ""
  ? html`<p><em>Start typing above to look up a firm's crop-by-crop footprint.</em></p>`
  : !firmMatch
    ? html`<p><em>No exact match for "${firmPick}". Pick a suggestion from the dropdown.</em></p>`
    : html`<div>
        <p><strong>${firmMatch.Firm}</strong> holds ${firmMatch["Total certificates"]} certificates
        across ${firmMatch["Crops active in"]} crop(s), ${firmMatch["Share of all private certificates (%)"]}%
        of all private certificates nationally, ranked #${firmMatch.Rank} among all private applicants.</p>
        <div class="firm-lookup-table">${
          Inputs.table(firmCropRows, {
            columns: ["Crop", "Certificates", "% of this firm's own certificates",
                      "% of all private certificates in this crop"],
            rows: 15,
            sort: "Certificates",
            reverse: true
          })
        }</div>
      </div>`
```

---

::: {.callout-tip appearance="minimal"}
For the headline findings, the cotton case study, and full methodology, see
[Full analysis](analysis.qmd). Pipeline source: [project repository](https://github.com/ambika-subash/pvp-certification-explorer).
:::

::: {style="margin-top:1.5rem;padding:1.25rem 1.5rem;background:#f9fafb;border:1px solid #e5e7eb;border-radius:8px;font-size:0.82rem;color:#4b5563;line-height:1.7"}
**License.** Code: [MIT License](https://github.com/ambika-subash/pvp-certification-explorer/blob/main/LICENSE).
Data and content: [CC BY 4.0](https://github.com/ambika-subash/pvp-certification-explorer/blob/main/LICENSE-DATA).

**How to cite.** Subash, Ambika. 2026. *Who owns India's plant varieties? PVP
Certification Explorer (India).* Centre for Economic Studies and Planning, Jawaharlal
Nehru University. <https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.21140353>

<details><summary style="cursor:pointer;display:inline-block;padding:2px 10px;margin-top:0.25rem;background:#eef2f7;border-radius:4px;font-weight:600;color:#374151;font-size:0.78rem">Show BibTeX</summary>

```
@misc{subash2026pvp,
  author      = {Subash, Ambika},
  title       = {Who owns {India}'s plant varieties? {PVP} Certification Explorer ({India})},
  year        = {2026},
  institution = {Centre for Economic Studies and Planning, Jawaharlal Nehru University},
  doi         = {10.5281/zenodo.21140353},
  url         = {https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.21140353},
  note        = {Data updated weekly. Accessed: [date]}
}
```

</details>

**About the author.** Built by Ambika Subash, Centre for Economic Studies and Planning,
Jawaharlal Nehru University. Her research examines the political economy of agricultural
biotechnology regulation, seed systems, and regulatory governance in India.
[Substack](https://theloneresearcher.substack.com/) |
[ORCID](https://orcid.org/0000-0002-8636-7123) |
[Google Scholar](https://scholar.google.com/citations?user=QbSSDTAAAAAJ&hl=en)
:::
